sample.claim: Many early diagnostic AI systems were trained almost entirely on images from large research hospitals in wealthy countries
AI Standard of Care Misdiagnosis Case Tracker documents ongoing and closed cases of diagnostic AI failures, lawsuits, and litigation
Amazon trained the model on ten years of its own hiring history
downgraded CVs containing words like 'women's chess club' and scored graduates of all-women's colleges lower
if you train a model on past human decisions, and those decisions were discriminatory, the model replicates the discrimination
The technique used for film special effects and video dubbing
DHS report notes that deepfake tools are no longer expensive or technically complex
person with no technical background can create a convincing deepfake video from a handful of photographs
AI inherits and amplifies existing human problems — diagnostic AI learned from data reflecting historical inequity; Amazon's tool from discriminatory hiring history
A system that is '95% accurate' is wrong 1 in 20 times. In healthcare at scale, that is an enormous number of patients.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined at first appearance with bold-term / plain-meaning format: 'AI failure' (line 11), 'Diagnostic AI' (line 82), 'Training data bias' (line 88), 'algorithmic bias' (line 106), 'Deepfake' (line 120). Acronym 'DHS' expanded to 'U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS)' at line 124. 'deep learning' at line 122 is a mention-and-defer pointer, not a cold undefined relied-upon term — no knowledge of it is assumed. No hedging language found. Sentences average well under 25 words. Concrete examples precede abstractions throughout (skin-tone example before training data bias definition; Amazon story before algorithmic bias definition).

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences; most are 2-4 sentences. Five-question Worked Example is a numbered list (lines 147-162). DHS harm categories are a numbered list (lines 126-129). Healthcare and Hiring harm consequences are bullet lists. Concrete inline examples appear in Key Concepts sections for all three domains — not deferred to Worked Example only. All definitions follow bold-term / plain-meaning dash pattern.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week references, or grade weights appear anywhere in the reading prose.
